title: Why validate on the backend too?
prompt_md: Your React form already validates every field before submitting. Why should the backend **still** validate the same request?
options: It shouldn't — duplicating validation is wasted effort once the frontend checks it
Because the client cannot be trusted: anyone can bypass the UI with curl, Postman, scripts, or an outdated app version, so the backend is the real correctness and security boundary
Because backend validation makes the API respond faster than frontend validation
Because browsers strip validation rules before sending the request over HTTPS
explanation_md: Frontend validation is a UX feature — fast feedback, fewer round-trips — but it can be trivially bypassed with `curl`, Postman, a script, or a stale mobile build. The backend is the only place you control, so it must treat every request as potentially malformed or hostile. "Never trust the client" is the core rule. The other options are wrong: validation isn't redundant, it isn't a speed optimization, and HTTPS only encrypts traffic — it doesn't enforce your business rules.
